pattern: A single swap can only ever change a chosen substring's own one-vs-zero balance by exactly two (swapping one of its characters for the opposite type sitting outside it), so the answer is the best of three prefix-balance searches: already balanced, two ones too many with a spare zero available outside, or two zeros too many with a spare one available outside.
hints: Track the running difference between ones and zeros seen so far; a substring is already balanced exactly when this running value repeats, so the classic first-occurrence-of-each-value trick finds the longest such substring with zero swaps.

Any single swap that helps a chosen substring must exchange one of its own characters for the opposite type sitting outside it, which shifts that substring's own ones-minus-zeros balance by exactly plus or minus two -- so also look for the longest substring whose running-difference values differ by exactly 2, and separately by exactly -2.

For the plus-two case you need at least one zero left over outside the substring, and since the substring's own zero count is completely determined by its length once the difference is fixed at 2, this becomes a simple cap on how long that substring is allowed to be -- so only accept a matching pair of positions whose gap does not exceed that cap (an analogous cap using the total one-count applies to the minus-two case).

edge_inputs.description: one swap plus choosing the right substring reaches length four
no swap or substring choice can ever balance an all-ones string
a single character can never be balanced
already perfectly balanced with no swap needed
the longest unconstrained plus-two-difference span would consume every zero, so a shorter span must be used instead
the longest unconstrained minus-two-difference span would consume every one, so a shorter span must be used instead
the whole string is already balanced, so the zero-swap case gives the true maximum

stress.description: random small strings, exhaustively cross-checkable against a brute-force oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 11))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: large random string near the full length bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
